title: CNA - C++ Reimplementation of the XNA 4.0 API
description: CNA is a modern C++ reimplementation of the XNA 4.0 API, built on SDL3 with pluggable rendering backends including SDL_Renderer, OpenGL (EasyGL), and bgfx.

title: About CNA
description: Learn what CNA is, why it exists, and what problem it solves - a native C++ reimplementation of the Microsoft XNA 4.0 API built on SDL3.

title: CNA Features
description: Explore CNA's key features: XNA 4.0 API compatibility, SDL3 foundation, pluggable rendering backends (SDL_Renderer, OpenGL, bgfx), cross-platform support, and Nova-3D foundation.

title: CNA Architecture
description: Explore CNA's layered architecture: game code, XNA-compatible API layer, CNA internal abstraction layer, and pluggable backend implementations for SDL3, OpenGL, and bgfx.

title: CNA Documentation
description: CNA documentation - getting started, building, platform support, rendering backends, XNA compatibility, roadmap, and FAQ.

title: CNA Tutorials
description: 100 step-by-step CNA tutorials covering everything from your first window to shipping a cross-platform C++ game. Beginner to expert.

title: CNA Demos
description: CNA demos - web builds, Android builds, desktop builds, and the Speedy Blupi port. Try CNA in action across multiple platforms.

title: CNA Showcase
description: CNA showcase - browser-playable demos, real-world game ports, XNA sample ports, and platform validation status.

title: CNA Roadmap
description: CNA development roadmap - from XNA 4.0 API implementation to Nova-3D integration. An honest long-term plan for a work-in-progress project.

title: Contact CNA
description: Contact and links for the CNA project - GitHub repository, YouTube channel, web builds, and author contact.

title: Contribute to CNA
description: How to contribute to CNA - build instructions, repository structure, code style, NOXNA marker, and open contribution opportunities.

title: CNA Videos
description: CNA video content - development updates, demos, and tutorials on YouTube.

title: Getting Started with CNA
description: Get started with CNA - prerequisites, first build, and a minimal XNA-style game skeleton in C++.

title: Building CNA
description: Complete CNA build instructions for Linux and Windows, all rendering backends, cross-compilation with MinGW-w64, and optional system SDL.

title: CNA Platform Support
description: CNA platform support matrix - Linux, Windows, Android, and Emscripten/WebAssembly. Current status and future targets.

title: CNA FAQ
description: Frequently asked questions about CNA - its goals, differences from FNA and MonoGame, C++ approach, and how to contribute.

title: XNA 4.0 API Compatibility
description: CNA XNA 4.0 API compatibility table - which classes, namespaces, and methods are implemented, partial, or planned.

title: CNA Rendering Backends
description: CNA rendering backends guide - SDL_Renderer, EasyGL (OpenGL), bgfx, and Vulkan. Comparison, tradeoffs, and how to select a backend.

title: CNA Effects System
description: CNA effects system documentation - BasicEffect, AlphaTestEffect, DualTextureEffect, EnvironmentMapEffect, SkinnedEffect, custom ShaderEffect, and effect interfaces.

title: CNA SpriteBatch
description: CNA SpriteBatch documentation - 2D sprite batching, Begin/End, Draw, DrawString, SpriteSortMode, BlendState, and SpriteEffects.

title: CNA Audio System
description: CNA audio system documentation - SoundEffect, SoundEffectInstance, DynamicSoundEffectInstance, MediaPlayer, Song, and XACT stub classes.

title: CNA Input System
description: CNA input system documentation - Keyboard, Mouse, GamePad, TouchPanel, TextInputEXT, and Accelerometer APIs built on SDL3.

title: CNA Math Types Reference
description: CNA math types reference - Vector2, Vector3, Vector4, Matrix, Quaternion, Color, Rectangle, Point, MathHelper, BoundingBox, BoundingSphere, BoundingFrustum, Plane, Ray, Curve.

title: CNA ContentManager
description: CNA ContentManager documentation - loading typed assets with Load<T>(), built-in type readers, JSON descriptor formats, custom ContentTypeReader, and XNB pipeline note.

title: CNA 3D Rendering
description: CNA 3D rendering guide - vertex buffers, index buffers, draw calls, BasicEffect, camera setup, depth state, Model rendering, instancing, and render targets.

title: CNA PackedVector Types
description: CNA PackedVector types reference - all 17 XNA 4.0 packed vector types including HalfSingle, HalfVector2, HalfVector4, NormalizedByte4, Rgba1010102, and the IEEE 754 half-float implementation.

title: CNA vs FNA vs MonoGame
description: Compare CNA with FNA, MonoGame, and original XNA 4.0 — feature matrix, platform support, API coverage, and guidance on when to choose each library.

title: Migrating from MonoGame to CNA
description: Step-by-step guide for migrating a MonoGame or XNA 4.0 game to CNA (C++23). Namespace mapping, C++ idioms, memory management, content pipeline differences, and known gaps.

title: CNA Game Loop and Lifecycle
description: CNA Game class and lifecycle documentation - constructor, Initialize, LoadContent, Update, Draw, UnloadContent, GameTime, GraphicsDeviceManager, GameWindow, and GameComponent.

title: CNA Graphics State
description: CNA graphics state documentation - BlendState, DepthStencilState, RasterizerState, SamplerState, Viewport, and ScissorRectangle.

title: CNA Model Loading
description: CNA model loading and rendering documentation - Model class API, .model.json descriptor format, ModelMesh, ModelMeshPart, skeletal animation, and ContentManager integration.

title: CNA Video Playback
description: CNA video playback documentation - VideoPlayer, Video class, FFmpeg integration, platform availability, and rendering patterns.

title: CNA Storage
description: CNA storage documentation - StorageDevice, StorageContainer, and StorageDeviceNotConnectedException for save-game and user-data file I/O.

title: CNA Sensors
description: CNA sensor documentation - Accelerometer API for tilt and shake detection, AccelerometerState, platform support, and future gyroscope/compass plans.

title: CNA Shader Effects
description: CNA ShaderEffect documentation - user-supplied GLSL and SPIR-V custom shaders loaded via .shader.json, EffectParameter uniforms, and backend availability.

title: CNA Render Targets
description: CNA RenderTarget2D documentation - off-screen rendering, FBO/Vulkan backends, multiple render targets, RenderTargetCube, post-processing pipelines.

title: Nova-3D and CNA
description: How CNA serves as the internal runtime and rendering abstraction layer for the planned Nova-3D engine - architecture, responsibilities, and current status.

title: CNA Docs Roadmap
description: Detailed CNA development roadmap - current sprint, milestones, and long-term plans including Nova-3D integration.

title: Tutorial 01: Introduction to CNA
description: Learn what CNA is, how it relates to XNA and MonoGame, and what you can build with this C++23 game framework.

title: Tutorial 02: Setting Up Your Dev Environment
description: Install GCC/Clang C++23, CMake, SDL3, clone CNA and sharp-runtime, build CNA, and run the 374-test suite.

title: Tutorial 03: Your First CNA Window
description: Create a CMake project, write the minimal CNA Game class, link CNA, and open a cornflower-blue window.

title: Tutorial 04: The Game Class Lifecycle
description: Understand the order CNA calls Initialize, LoadContent, Update, Draw, UnloadContent, and OnExiting in your Game subclass.

title: Tutorial 05: The Game Loop
description: Understand GameTime, fixed vs variable timestep, IsFixedTimeStep, TargetElapsedTime, and frame-rate-independent movement in CNA.

title: Tutorial 06: Drawing Your First 2D Shape
description: Use SpriteBatch and a 1x1 white Texture2D to draw filled colored rectangles in CNA.

title: Tutorial 07: Working with Colors
description: Master the CNA Color struct: predefined colors, RGBA construction, color arithmetic, lerping, and alpha blending.

title: Tutorial 08: Loading and Drawing Textures
description: Load PNG textures with ContentManager, draw with position, source rect, color, rotation, origin, scale, and SpriteEffects in CNA.

title: Tutorial 09: Drawing Text with SpriteFont
description: Load SpriteFont from a font.json descriptor, draw text, measure string size, and centre text on screen in CNA.

title: Tutorial 10: Handling Keyboard Input
description: Read keyboard state in CNA with Keyboard::GetState(), detect key presses and releases, and move a player with WASD.

title: Tutorial 11: Handling Mouse Input
description: Read mouse position, button state, and scroll wheel in CNA with Mouse::GetState(), detect clicks, and drag objects.

title: Tutorial 12: Moving Sprites and Basic Animation
description: Move sprites with Vector2 velocity, apply boundary checking, smooth delta-time movement, rotation, and scaling in CNA.

title: Tutorial 13: Sprite Sheets and Frame Animation
description: Animate a sprite by cycling source rectangles on a sprite sheet in CNA, including an AnimationPlayer helper class.

title: Tutorial 14: Playing Sound Effects
description: Learn how to load and play sound effects in CNA using SoundEffect, SoundEffectInstance, and 3D positional audio.

title: Tutorial 15: Playing Background Music
description: Learn how to stream and control background music in CNA using MediaPlayer, Song, and the MediaState enum.

title: Tutorial 16: Basic Collision Detection
description: Learn Rectangle, circle, and bounding-volume collision detection in CNA using XNA-compatible types.

title: Tutorial 17: 2D Camera and Viewport
description: Implement a 2D scrolling camera in CNA using Matrix transforms, SpriteBatch.Begin(transformMatrix), and Viewport clamping.

title: Tutorial 18: Game States - Menus and Scenes
description: Implement a state machine with menus, gameplay, and fade transitions in CNA using a stack-based GameState manager.

title: Tutorial 19: Saving and Loading Data
description: Save and load game data in CNA using StorageDevice, StorageContainer, and JSON serialization across Linux, Windows, Android, and Web.

title: Tutorial 20: Building and Running Your Game
description: Build your CNA game for Linux, Windows, WebAssembly (Emscripten), and Android using CMake release configs and packaging targets.

title: Tutorial 21: SpriteBatch Deep Dive
description: Master SpriteBatch in CNA: all Begin() overloads, SpriteSortMode, every Draw() variant, DrawString, and batching performance.

title: Tutorial 22: Blend Modes and Alpha Compositing
description: Master BlendState presets, custom blend states, additive blending for particles, and pre-multiplied vs straight alpha in CNA.

title: Tutorial 23: Render Targets for Off-Screen Rendering
description: Use RenderTarget2D in CNA to render scenes off-screen, create minimaps, UI canvases, and set up post-processing pipelines.

title: Tutorial 24: Post-Processing with Render Targets
description: Add grayscale, bloom, and vignette screen-space post-processing effects to your CNA game using render targets and GLSL shaders.

title: Tutorial 25: SpriteSortMode and Layering
description: Understand all five SpriteSortMode values, layer depth, performance trade-offs, and multi-batch layering strategies in CNA.

title: Tutorial 26: Tilemaps and Tile-Based Worlds
description: Build a tile-based world in CNA: TileMap class, atlas rendering with source rectangles, tile collision maps, and Tiled JSON export.

title: Tutorial 27: Scrolling Backgrounds and Parallax
description: Learn how to implement parallax scrolling backgrounds with multiple layers in CNA using C++.

title: Tutorial 28: Particle Systems in 2D
description: Build a 2D particle system in CNA: particle pools, emitter patterns, additive blending, and fire/explosion effects.

title: Tutorial 29: UI Elements - Buttons and Menus
description: Build interactive buttons and menus in CNA with mouse hit-testing, hover/click states, and keyboard navigation.

title: Tutorial 30: GamePad and Controller Input
description: Learn how to read gamepad and controller input in CNA using GamePad::GetState, thumbsticks, buttons, triggers, and vibration.

title: Tutorial 31: Your First 3D Triangle
description: Render your first 3D triangle in CNA using VertexBuffer, BasicEffect, and DrawPrimitives with World/View/Projection matrices.

title: Tutorial 32: BasicEffect and 3D Lighting
description: Master BasicEffect in CNA: directional lights, ambient, diffuse, emissive, texture, and vertex colour modes for 3D lighting.

title: Tutorial 33: Matrices and Transformations
description: Learn how World, View, and Projection matrices work in CNA, and how to compose transforms for a solar system demo.

title: Tutorial 34: 3D Camera Setup and Control
description: Build an FPS camera with WASD movement and mouse look using CNA's Matrix and input APIs.

title: Tutorial 35: Loading 3D Models
description: Load and draw 3D models in CNA using ContentManager, the Model class, and BasicEffect transforms.

title: Tutorial 36: Texturing 3D Models
description: Apply textures to 3D models in CNA using BasicEffect, UV coordinates, SamplerState filtering, and mipmaps.

title: Tutorial 37: Multiple Light Sources
description: Use BasicEffect's 3 directional lights, simulate point lights, and understand CNA lighting architecture.

title: Tutorial 38: Vertex Buffers and Index Buffers
description: Create static and dynamic VertexBuffers, use IndexBuffers with DrawIndexedPrimitives, and build deformable meshes in CNA.

title: Tutorial 39: Depth Buffer and Z-Fighting
description: Understand depth buffers, DepthStencilState, z-fighting causes and fixes, transparent object sorting, and the skybox depth trick in CNA.

title: Tutorial 40: Primitive Types
description: Learn PrimitiveType enum, winding order, DrawPrimitives vs DrawIndexedPrimitives, and triangle count calculation in CNA.

title: Tutorial 41: Vector2, Vector3, Vector4 Math
description: Master Vector2, Vector3, and Vector4 operations in CNA: dot product, cross product, normalize, lerp, reflect, and transform.

title: Tutorial 42: Matrix Operations and Transforms
description: Learn Matrix identity, multiply, transpose, invert, decompose, CreateShadow, CreateBillboard, and transform points in CNA.

title: Tutorial 43: Quaternions for Smooth Rotation
description: Learn quaternion rotation in CNA: CreateFromAxisAngle, Slerp, compose rotations, gimbal lock avoidance, and spacecraft controls.

title: Tutorial 44: Bounding Volumes and Spatial Queries
description: Use BoundingBox, BoundingSphere, and BoundingFrustum in CNA for intersection tests, frustum culling, and spatial queries.

title: Tutorial 45: ContentManager and Asset Pipeline
description: Load and unload game assets with ContentManager in CNA: Texture2D, Model, SoundEffect, SpriteFont, JSON descriptors, and state-based unloading.

title: Tutorial 46: Custom ContentTypeReader
description: Create custom ContentTypeReader implementations in CNA to load arbitrary file types like level data from JSON through the ContentManager pipeline.

title: Tutorial 47: GameComponent and DrawableGameComponent
description: Build modular game systems with GameComponent and DrawableGameComponent in CNA, including FPS counter, input manager, and debug overlays.

title: Tutorial 48: Fixed vs Variable Timestep
description: Understand IsFixedTimeStep, TargetElapsedTime, and interpolation in CNA for stable physics and smooth rendering.

title: Tutorial 49: Touch Input and Gestures
description: Handle touch input, TouchLocation states, and gesture recognition (drag, pinch-to-zoom) with CNA's TouchPanel API on SDL3.

title: Tutorial 50: Accelerometer and Sensors
description: Read accelerometer data in CNA for tilt-based controls: Accelerometer.Start/Stop, AccelerometerReading, platform support notes, and a marble game example.

title: Tutorial 51: Custom Vertex Types
description: Define custom vertex types in CNA with IVertexType, VertexDeclaration, and VertexElement for position, normal, texcoord, and tangent data.

title: Tutorial 52: Writing Custom Shaders (ShaderEffect)
description: Write GLSL custom shaders in CNA using ShaderEffect, .shader.json descriptors, Apply(), and the EASYGL and Vulkan backend pipeline.

title: Tutorial 53: GLSL Uniforms and EffectParameter
description: Learn how EffectParameter bridges C++ and GLSL uniforms in CNA, covering SetValue overloads, EffectParameterCollection, array passing, and UBO update patterns.

title: Tutorial 54: AlphaTestEffect and Alpha Masking
description: Use AlphaTestEffect in CNA for efficient alpha masking: AlphaFunction enum, ReferenceAlpha threshold, vertex colour support, foliage rendering, and comparison with alpha blending.

title: Tutorial 55: DualTextureEffect for Lightmaps
description: Combine a diffuse albedo texture with a precomputed lightmap using DualTextureEffect in CNA. Learn VertexPositionDualTexture, the baked lightmap workflow, and performance characteristics.

title: Tutorial 56: EnvironmentMapEffect for Reflections
description: Add cubemap-based reflections to 3D objects in CNA with EnvironmentMapEffect. Learn FresnelFactor, EnvironmentMapAmount, specular highlights, and TextureCube loading.

title: Tutorial 57: SkinnedEffect and Skeletal Animation
description: Drive GPU skeletal animation in CNA with SkinnedEffect. Learn bone matrix palettes, SetBoneTransforms, vertex blend weights, animation clip playback, and the bone hierarchy update loop.

title: Tutorial 58: Normal Mapping
description: Implement per-pixel normal mapping in CNA using a custom GLSL shader with TBN matrix construction, tangent-space normal decoding, and EffectParameter texture binding.

title: Tutorial 59: Shadow Mapping
description: Implement real-time shadow mapping in CNA with a two-pass depth render, light-space matrix, PCF soft shadows, and shadow acne bias. Full GLSL and C++ draw loop included.

title: Tutorial 60: Instanced Rendering
description: Draw thousands of identical meshes in a single GPU draw call using DrawInstancedPrimitives, a second per-instance VertexBuffer, and GLSL instanced vertex shaders in CNA.

title: Tutorial 61: Occlusion Queries
description: Use OcclusionQuery in CNA to count GPU-visible fragments and gate expensive effects like lens flare, light shafts, and hardware LOD selection without CPU stalls.

title: Tutorial 62: Multiple Render Targets (MRT)
description: Use SetRenderTargets in CNA to write to multiple textures simultaneously in one draw call, enabling deferred rendering G-buffer passes, SSAO, and multi-pass effects.

title: Tutorial 63: Stencil Buffer Effects
description: Master DepthStencilState in CNA: write and read the stencil buffer to create object outlines, portal rendering, mirror reflections, and shadow volume effects.

title: Tutorial 64: Cubemaps and Skyboxes
description: Load and render TextureCube skyboxes in CNA using an inverted cube mesh, samplerCube GLSL sampling, and stripped-translation view matrices for background environment rendering.

title: Tutorial 65: MSAA Anti-Aliasing
description: Enable MSAA anti-aliasing in CNA via GraphicsDeviceManager.PreferMultiSampling, choose sample counts, and implement FXAA as a post-process alternative for unsupported backends.

title: Tutorial 66: Post-Processing: Blur and Bloom
description: Implement Gaussian blur and bloom post-processing in CNA using RenderTarget chains, bright-pass thresholding, and additive composite blending.

title: Tutorial 67: Deferred Rendering
description: Implement deferred rendering in CNA with a G-Buffer geometry pass and a full-screen lighting pass supporting many dynamic lights.

title: Tutorial 68: Terrain Rendering
description: Load a heightmap, build a vertex/index buffer, calculate normals, and render textured terrain with BasicEffect in CNA.

title: Tutorial 69: Water and Reflections
description: Implement planar reflections, animated normal-map water, clip planes, and Fresnel blending in CNA.

title: Tutorial 70: Procedural Geometry
description: Build meshes at runtime in CNA: procedural spheres, cylinders, noise terrain, dynamic VertexBuffer updates, and a marching cubes overview.

title: Tutorial 71: Memory Management in C++
description: RAII resource ownership, unique_ptr asset holders, Dispose patterns, move semantics, and common pitfalls in CNA C++ game development.

title: Tutorial 72: Choosing the Right Rendering Backend
description: Compare SDL_RENDERER, EASYGL, VULKAN, and BGFX backends for CNA games: feature coverage, performance, and how to select one at build time.

title: Tutorial 73: Performance Profiling and Optimization
description: Profile and optimise CNA games: FPS counters, draw call counting, texture atlases, SpriteBatch batching rules, RenderDoc, and Vulkan validation layers.

title: Tutorial 74: Frustum Culling
description: Use BoundingFrustum and BoundingSphere in CNA to cull invisible objects before submitting draw calls, reducing GPU load significantly.

title: Tutorial 75: Level of Detail (LOD)
description: Implement distance-based LOD in CNA with multiple mesh variants, impostor sprites, hysteresis to prevent popping, and an OcclusionQuery overview.

title: Tutorial 76: Spatial Partitioning
description: Implement uniform grids, quadtrees, and octrees in CNA for efficient range, ray, and frustum queries in large game worlds.

title: Tutorial 77: Asset Streaming and Async Loading
description: Eliminate loading stalls in CNA games with std::async background loading, a thread-safe resource queue, and a loading screen pattern.

title: Tutorial 78: Multi-threading Considerations
description: Safely use worker threads in CNA games: job queue with std::jthread, lockless ring buffer, game logic on workers, GPU calls on main, and SDL3_mixer audio threading.

title: Tutorial 79: Debugging Rendering Issues
description: Techniques for debugging CNA rendering: wireframe mode, RenderDoc, OcclusionQuery, vertex buffer validation, and SDL3 debug output.

title: Tutorial 80: Cross-Platform Build Guide
description: Building CNA on Windows, Linux, macOS, Android, and WebAssembly. CMake toolchains, #ifdef guards, path handling, and SDL3 platform abstraction.

title: Tutorial 81: Emscripten: Building for WebAssembly
description: Build and deploy CNA games for WebAssembly using Emscripten. Covers SDL3 target, WebGL 2 via EASYGL, emscripten_set_main_loop, async file loading, and the shell HTML template.

title: Tutorial 82: Android Build and Deployment
description: Build and deploy CNA games on Android. NDK setup, SDL3 Android template, OpenGL ES 3.0, TouchPanel, Accelerometer, and APK packaging.

title: Tutorial 83: Migrating from MonoGame/FNA
description: Step-by-step guide to migrating a MonoGame or FNA C# game to CNA C++. Covers namespace changes, C# to C++ type mapping, memory management, content pipeline, and audio.

title: Tutorial 84: Migrating from XNA to CNA
description: Migrating legacy XNA 4.0 C# games to CNA C++. Identical API surface goal, C++ semantics, NOXNA markers, XACT stub, content pipeline, and the 374-test validation suite.

title: Tutorial 85: Vulkan Backend - Setup and Quirks
description: Using CNA's Vulkan rendering backend. ~93% XNA coverage, best performance. Vulkan SDK, validation layers, SPIR-V shaders, and differences from EasyGL.

title: Tutorial 86: bgfx Backend - Features and Limitations
description: CNA's bgfx rendering backend. ~82% XNA coverage. What works, what's limited, and when to choose bgfx over EasyGL or Vulkan.

title: Tutorial 87: Writing a Custom Rendering Backend
description: Extend CNA with a custom rendering backend. IGraphicsBackend interface, required methods, texture upload, shader compilation, state management, and registering the backend.

title: Tutorial 88: Nova-3D - Building a Game Engine on CNA
description: Nova-3D is a game engine built on CNA as its runtime. Learn how CNA provides the rendering/input/audio foundation and what Nova-3D adds on top.

title: Tutorial 89: Extending CNA with sharp-runtime
description: sharp-runtime provides C#-style type aliases and patterns for CNA. IDisposable, IEquatable, Event<T>, ListCS<T>, and using it independently.

title: Tutorial 90: Integration with easy-gl Directly
description: When and how to bypass CNA and call easy-gl (OpenGL ES 3.0/3.2 wrapper) directly for custom render paths, compute shaders, and interop with CNA render state.

title: Tutorial 91: Building a 2D Platformer
description: Build a 2D platformer in CNA. Player/Platform/Level classes, AABB collision, gravity, one-way platforms, coyote time, input buffering, animation state machine, and parallax background.

title: Tutorial 92: Building a Top-Down RPG
description: Build a top-down RPG in CNA. Tilemap world, 8-directional player movement, NPCs, dialogue, inventory, camera following player, and Y-sorting sprites.

title: Tutorial 93: Building a First-Person 3D Game
description: Build a first-person 3D game in CNA. FPS camera with mouse look and WASD, weapon model in view space, raycasting hit detection, enemy AI state machine, minimap on RenderTarget2D, and HUD overlay.

title: Tutorial 94: Building a Puzzle Game
description: Build a match-3 puzzle game in CNA. Grid-based board, piece matching logic, Lerp animations, undo/redo stack, save state, score/timer, and particle effects on match.

title: Tutorial 95: Speedy Blupi - A Real-World CNA Port
description: How Speedy Blupi was ported from MonoGame to CNA. API compatibility lessons, content pipeline adaptation, performance results, and key porting patterns.

title: Tutorial 96: Physics Integration (Box2D / Bullet)
description: Integrate Box2D v3 for 2D physics and Bullet Physics for 3D physics with CNA. Physics step in Update, syncing bodies to CNA transforms, and debug drawing.

title: Tutorial 97: Networking and Multiplayer Basics
description: CNA has no networking API. Add multiplayer using BSD sockets, ASIO, ENet, or SDL3_net. Lockstep vs client-server, state serialization, and latency hiding.

title: Tutorial 98: Localization and Multiple Languages
description: Add localization to CNA games. JSON string tables, SpriteFont Unicode, glyph ranges, RTL text challenges, system locale detection, and hot-reload in debug.

title: Tutorial 99: Unit Testing CNA Game Logic
description: Test your CNA game logic with GoogleTest. CNA's 374-test suite, headless CNA init, testing math and ECS separately from rendering, mocking IDisposable resources, and CI integration.

title: Tutorial 100: Shipping Your CNA Game
description: Release builds, asset bundling, Windows NSIS/WiX installer, Linux AppImage/Flatpak, web deployment, Android Play Store, license compliance, and CPack packaging.
